specific_location: Barr Lake State Park banding station
description: The crown was red, and the upperparts were reddish brown with a comparatively dull, olive-brown rump, uppertail coverts, and tail. The underparts were white with large, oval, blackish brown spots extending from the upper breast down to the belly with the largest concentration of spots along the flanks. The broken eye rings were thick and white. The bird's bill was large relative to Catharus sp. The maxilla was black, and the mandible was pink with a blackish gray tip. The wing chord measured 105 mm and the tail 70 mm. The difference between the length of the longest primary covert and primary 10 was 2.1 mm. The difference between the lengths of primaries 8 and 6 was 2.6 mm. These measurements fall within the ranges appropriate for a Wood Thrush, according to published data. This bird was aged as Hatching Year due to the incomplete pneumaticization of its skull.Behaviors: Normal behaviors of the Wood Thrush were not observed during its capture and banding.Call: not applicablePlumage: upperprts reddish brown underprts white
photographer: Photographer: Jessica Lux, Jessica Lux, Jessica Lux, Jessica Lux
